# Sandcastle AI Agent Instructions

## What this repository is

Sandcastle is an Attack & Defense CTF infrastructure scaffold for local Docker-based iteration. It provides a deterministic per-team Docker network, SSH gateway containers, and a bundled vulnerable service template, but intentionally does not bundle scoreboard or gameserver logic.

## Key files and directories

- `README.md` — project overview and usage.
- `docs/architecture.md` — topology, contract, and design intent.
- `scripts/setup.sh` — source of truth for generated team directories and `docker-compose.yml`.
- `scripts/gen_compose.py` — compatibility wrapper that delegates to `scripts/setup.sh`.
- `docker-compose.yml` — generated by `scripts/setup.sh`, do not edit directly.
- `scripts/setup.sh`, `scripts/start.sh`, `scripts/stop.sh`, `scripts/reset.sh` — orchestration helpers.
- `docker/ssh/Dockerfile` — reusable team SSH gateway image.
- `teams/generated/team<N>/service/` — ignored generated per-team copy of the vulnerable service template.
- `services/README.md` — contract for adding future vulnerable services.
- `services/example-vuln/` — example vulnerable app source and image builder.

## Development workflow

- Use `scripts/setup.sh --teams N` to generate team directories and `docker-compose.yml` for N teams.
- Use `./scripts/start.sh` or `docker compose up --build` to start the scaffold.
- Use `./scripts/stop.sh` to stop while preserving data.
- Use `./scripts/reset.sh` to recreate containers and volumes.

## Important conventions

- `docker-compose.yml` is generated by `scripts/setup.sh`. Change the generator, not the generated file.
- The scaffold does not require `VULN_IMAGE`; each `team<N>-vuln` service is built from `teams/generated/team<N>/service`.
- Each team gets:
  - `team<N>-ssh` gateway at `10.10.<N>.2` with host port `2200+N` mapped to SSH.
  - `team<N>-vuln` service at `10.10.<N>.3`.
- Team SSH gateways mount `/var/run/docker.sock`, so they can control Docker on the host. This is intentionally for local iteration and is not a security boundary.

## Service contract

Future vulnerable app images must support these environment variables and mounts:

- `TEAM_ID=<N>`
- `TEAM_NAME=Team <N>`
- `SERVICE_PORT=8080`
- mounted volume `/app/data`

The current infrastructure does not assume a specific protocol or flag format.

## When editing the codebase

- For infrastructure changes, update `scripts/setup.sh` first.
- For vulnerable app work, add or extend content under `services/` and update `services/README.md` if needed.
- For SSH gateway behavior, edit `docker/ssh/Dockerfile`.
- Keep the infrastructure and challenge/service layers separate wherever possible.

## Useful facts for code changes

- `ctf-network` is a Docker bridge network on `10.10.0.0/16`.
- SSH gateways are built from `docker/ssh/Dockerfile` and bind-mount that team's generated service source tree.
- Team SSH login credentials are `team<N>` with password `team<N>pass`.

## Recommended agent behavior

- Prefer using existing scripts and documented workflow instead of inventing ad hoc compose or Docker commands.
- When asked to add features, preserve the scaffold philosophy: infrastructure only, with a reusable vulnerable app contract.
- When asked to fix or extend networking, keep the deterministic IP address allocation pattern.
- When asked to add new commands or docs, link back to `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, and `services/README.md` rather than duplicating full explanations.

## Agent Clarification Policy

- Always ask for clarifications when any requirement, constraint, or desired behavior is ambiguous or incomplete.
- Do not make unilateral design or policy decisions unless the user explicitly specifies them. When in doubt, present reasonable options and ask which to choose.
- If the user provides an explicit default or permission to decide, agents may proceed using that guidance and should mention the chosen default in their response.

- See the clarification skill for a short interactive template: `.github/skills/clarification-skill.md`.
